name: Proximity-based alignment with leftNormal spatial verification
description: Tests that the skill produces the correct two-step alignment algorithm: proximity direction detection followed by multi-sample leftNormal spatial verification
prompt: Convert lanelet2_map.osm (in the fixtures directory) to RRHD format. Write output to aligned_map.rrhd. After conversion, display the EXACT alignment algorithm code you used — show: (1) the proximity-based direction detection comparing d_starts vs d_cross distances, and (2) the leftNormal spatial verification using 90-degree CCW normal with dot product at multiple sample points. Copy-paste those code sections verbatim from your script.

assertions.description: Pipeline runs without error and produces .rrhd file
Proximity-based direction detection compares start distances vs cross distances
Uses leftNormal (90-degree CCW normal) for spatial verification
Dot product used in spatial verification
Does not use a simple overall direction dot product (fails on curves)
Multi-sample spatial verification, not single-point
assertions.expected: The alignment code must verify spatial left-side correctness at MULTIPLE sample points along the lane (e.g., linspace to pick 3-5 indices, or a loop over sample points), using local tangent at each point — NOT a single overall direction vector. Evidence: a loop or vectorized operation over multiple sample indices with local tangent computation at each. NEGATIVE: a single dot product of overall boundary direction against overall lane direction is WRONG and must fail this assertion.

name: Sign assets resolve to .svg paths not .fbx
description: Tests that Lanelet2 sign subtype codes map to RoadRunner .svg sign asset paths, not .fbx
prompt: Convert lanelet2_map.osm (in the fixtures directory) to RRHD format. Write output to signs_map.rrhd. After conversion, display: (1) the sign asset mapping code showing how Lanelet2 subtype codes are resolved to RoadRunner asset paths, and (2) list every sign asset path that was assigned. Show the actual path strings.

assertions.description: Pipeline runs without error and produces .rrhd file
Sign asset paths use .svg extension
Does not use .fbx for sign assets (common base-model mistake)
Sign paths follow structured naming pattern from Lanelet2 subtype codes
assertions.expected: Sign asset paths must follow a recognizable structured naming pattern such as Sign_330-A.svg (Japanese), R1-1_Stop.svg, or Sign_R2-1.svg (MUTCD). Must NOT use made-up generic paths like Stop_JP_01.svg, TrafficSign.svg, or Danger_JP.svg. The mapping must go FROM Lanelet2 subtype codes (e.g., speed_sign, stop_sign, yield) TO specific RoadRunner sign file names. NEGATIVE: Generic invented path names that don't correspond to real RoadRunner sign assets must fail.

name: Boundary geometry is immutable — no resampling or flipping
description: Tests that boundary geometry is stored exactly as parsed from OSM nodes and never modified
prompt: Convert lanelet2_map.osm (in the fixtures directory) to RRHD format. Write output to immutable_map.rrhd. After conversion, display the code that: (1) assigns boundary geometry from parsed OSM way coordinates, and (2) show evidence that boundaries are NOT resampled, flipped, or modified. Also show how shared boundaries are deduplicated via wayToBndID map.

assertions.description: Pipeline runs without error
No boundary resampling (interp1/pchip on boundary geometry)
No flipud/flip on boundary geometry
Uses wayToBndID or equivalent deduplication map for shared boundaries
Boundary geometry assigned directly from parsed coordinates without modification
assertions.expected: Evidence that boundary Geometry is assigned directly from the parsed OSM way node coordinates (e.g., geomMap(wayID), wayGeom, wayCoords). The code must NOT resample, flip, project, or otherwise modify boundary points. Alignment differences are handled via Alignment='Backward' property, NOT by modifying the geometry array. NEGATIVE: Any code that does interp1/pchip/resample/flipud/fliplr on boundary geometry, or that modifies boundary point arrays before assignment, must fail.

name: Reject standard OpenStreetMap file with proper explanation
description: Tests that the skill identifies non-Lanelet2 OSM and explains the limitation instead of attempting conversion
prompt: Convert the OpenStreetMap file standard_osm.osm (in the fixtures directory) to RRHD format. It contains standard highway=residential ways.

assertions.description: Identifies as standard OpenStreetMap (not Lanelet2)
Mentions Lanelet2 requirement
Does not attempt to write an RRHD file for non-Lanelet2 input
Explains WHY the file cannot be converted (identifies the format difference)
assertions.expected: The response must explain that the file lacks type=lanelet relations OR that it contains highway=* ways typical of standard OpenStreetMap (not Lanelet2). It must clearly communicate the limitation — not just refuse silently. NEGATIVE: Simply saying 'cannot convert' or 'error occurred' without explaining that Lanelet2 requires type=lanelet relations (which this file lacks) must fail.

name: Enforcement gates verify alignment, spatial, extension, and completeness
description: Tests that the mandatory enforcement gate runs before write() with all required checks
prompt: Convert lanelet2_map.osm (in the fixtures directory) to RRHD format. Write output to gated_map.rrhd. After conversion, display the EXACT enforcement gate code that runs before write(). Show all assert() calls and their conditions. The gate must include: spatial left-side verification, asset extension validation, and completeness checks.

assertions.description: Pipeline with enforcement gates runs without error
Spatial left-side check with multi-sample verification present
Asset extension validation present
Completeness check present
No simple lane-width-in-range check (base model approach, not skill approach)
Gates use assert() with meaningful error messages and run BEFORE write()
assertions.expected: The enforcement gate code must: (1) contain assert() calls (not just if/warning), (2) include descriptive error messages in the assert (e.g., 'SPATIAL ERROR', 'ASSET ERROR', 'INCOMPLETE'), and (3) execute BEFORE write(rrMap, ...). NEGATIVE: Gates that only check lane width ranges, or that run after write(), or that use warning() instead of assert() must fail.
